pattern: The ordering guarantee of a search tree means a value below the low bound rules out its entire left side, and a value above the high bound rules out its entire right side, so whole branches can be skipped without visiting them.
hints: If the current node's value is below the low bound, nothing in its left branch can be in range either, so only its right branch needs checking.
Symmetrically, if the value is above the high bound, only its left branch can possibly contribute.
Otherwise the node itself is in range, so add its value and recurse into both children.

edge_inputs.description: range covers a middle slice of the tree
a denser tree with a narrower range
single node exactly inside the range
single node entirely outside the range
low and high collapse onto a single node's value
the range sits entirely above every value in the tree
range spans the full constraint value space, covering every node

stress.description: random binary search tree from a shuffled permutation with a random in-range window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20000)))
    vals = rng.sample(range(1, 100001), n)
    order = vals[:]
    rng.shuffle(order)
    root = None
    nodes = {}
    def insert(v):
        nonlocal root
        if root is None:
            root = v
            nodes[v] = [None, None]
            return
        cur = root
        while True:
            l, r = nodes[cur]
            if v < cur:
                if l is None:
                    nodes[cur][0] = v
                    nodes[v] = [None, None]
                    return
                cur = l
            else:
                if r is None:
                    nodes[cur][1] = v
                    nodes[v] = [None, None]
                    return
                cur = r
    for v in order:
        insert(v)
    from collections import deque
    out = []
    q = deque([root])
    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        if cur is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(cur)
        cl, cr = nodes[cur]
        q.append(cl)
        q.append(cr)
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    a = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    b = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    low, high = min(a, b), max(a, b)
    return [out, low, high]

complexity.time: O(n) worst case, O(log n + k) for a balanced tree
